Spotlight: No worthwhile form as yet and instantly opposed.

RaceName: Moxy Chester EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Showed some promise when fifth of eight on her debut at Haydock (1m, good to firm) 11 weeks ago; should have plenty more to come now her stamina is being more fully tested.

RaceName: Moxy Chester EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: All starts over 1m; ran to a useful level in three runs in maiden/novice company; beaten 8l into ninth last time but that was the Sandringham Stakes at Royal Ascot; likely to stay this far and she's a leading contender; hood returns today.

RaceName: Moxy Chester EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Modest form in two 1m events this season; bred to stay this far and do better but others bring more pressing claims.

RaceName: Moxy Chester EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Mixed messages from her pedigree regarding this trip but there was clear promise amidst inexperience on her debut at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) and her late headway offered hope she would stay; much respected.
Dual bumper winner and a winning chaser; 3-13 over hurdles and rated 135; mark left unchanged after an odds-on win at Clonmel; smart sort but has never won a handicap.

Impressive winner of a Ballinrobe bumper for Andrew Slattery and moved to this yard after; won a Punchestown maiden hurdle last October before finishing fourth in a Fairyhouse Grade 2 novice; third in a conditions event at the Punchestown festival; relatively unexposed, plenty of weight for handicap debut.
Second to the top-class Kargese in a Grade 1 juvenile event in 2024; fair form on the Flat year; has become regressive over jumps; failed to take to chasing last winter.
Last five races for Gordon Elliott were over fences; has suitable ground for stable debut (won twice on good last summer); cheekpieces now added to the tongue-tie used in chases.

Dual bumper winner is 2-7 over hurdles; won a Tramore maiden on New Year's Day (2m1f, soft) and Ayr novice (2m, soft) the following month; failed to fire in the Martin Pipe at Cheltenham; chased home an easy winner at Roscommon last month; better ground should suit, judged on bumper form.

Dual-purpose; 3-12 over hurdles; solid Flat run at Navan (1m6f, heavy) in March; has failed to show his best form in three subsequent handicap hurdles outings; remains 7lb above last winning mark; seems best with at least some ease in the ground.
Ex-Henry de Bromhead; third in Listed handicap chase at Navan in December; lost form after; best runs have been on soft; sold for 28,000gns in March and makes stable debut here.
Has established a good record at this venue; gained a second course win last month, also twice runner-up here; this is a bit shorter than her optimum trip.
In good form last October/November, notching a maiden win at Wexford and second in a novice contest at Fairyhouse; risky proposition now after consecutive failures to complete.
Has lost his way since a fine second in the 2025 Topham; four non-completions from six starts; recent return to hurdling has failed to spark a revival in form.

Second off 2lb higher than this in a handicap hurdle at Tipperary last October (2m, good); form tailed off after; relished the switch to fences when scoring at Downpatrick in March; put a below-par Cork run behind him with a course handicap chase win in May; could feature off a mark 10lb lower than his revised chase rating.

Mostly reliable in the first half of last season; enjoyed a fine spell during which she notched two wins and two seconds; bolted up at Ballinrobe in May on her second start after a break; appears fortunate to have got away with a 6lb rise; strong chance with Harry Cobden booked.

Sole success achieved at odds-on in a maiden hurdle at this venue last autumn; placed twice from 11 starts on the Flat, the most recent when second over 2m at Tramore on Tuesday; trainer also runs Faceman.

Two hurdle wins last year, a maiden at this venue (good) and a three-runner novice event at Tramore (good); fell five out on chasing debut at Tramore in April; returned to hurdling to record a neck victory in a 3m contest here eight weeks ago; 4lb higher with useful 5lb claimed.

Three wins over hurdles, 1-15 over fences; second twice over fences on decent ground at Musselburgh in January, and third in a chase at Wexford last week; previously out of the money behind Ad Caelum in a handicap hurdle at this venue.

Maiden winner on good ground at Roscommon last summer; lame on only subsequent 2025 start; ran in a competitive race at Punchestown on handicap debut; relatively unexposed type; of some interest; first reserve.

RaceName: Amodil Group Novices' Hurdle (ARC Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Defied market opposition to run away with a 3m Market Rasen maiden on British/hurdling debut three weeks ago (good), with Saint Arion over 20l adrift; all or nothing profile in Ireland beforehand so no cast-iron certainty to back that up, but this race doesn't appear any stronger.

RaceName: Amodil Group Novices' Hurdle (ARC Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Converted a realistic opportunity at Newton Abbot latest (2m6f, good) for a first hurdling win in five tries; penalty demands more, but a tidier round of jumping than any to date could unlock further improvement.

RaceName: Amodil Group Novices' Hurdle (ARC Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Essentially improving with practice over hurdles, albeit drawing the ire of the stewards in posting a 24l fifth at Uttoxeter latest (2m, good); more appeal in moderate handicaps after this.

RaceName: Amodil Group Novices' Hurdle (ARC Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Didn't kick on once switched to the Flat following initial promise in bumpers for Brian Ellison; sold for 6,000gns since last run; out of a Listed winner over this sort of trip, but new yard does most of its winning in handicaps.

RaceName: Amodil Group Novices' Hurdle (ARC Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Either or both the class 2 company and drop back to 3m compromised him in Uttoxeter's Summer Cup latest (blinkers debuted there retained); untried over today's even shorter trip since November 2023 and over hurdles for longer still, so hard to see how this assignment plays to many strengths.

RaceName: Amodil Group Novices' Hurdle (ARC Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Outpaced fourth over 2m here in May and a clear non-stayer of a mile longer at Market Rasen since (20l to find with Kiwi De Cotte on that showing), so intermediate trip makes total sense; does need to jump better.

RaceName: Amodil Group Novices' Hurdle (ARC Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
OwnerName: DL Adams, JA Adams & G McPherson
Spotlight: Regressive RPRs since a Huntingdon bumper debut second (soft), beaten by halfway over 2m here latest; return to 2m4f isn't persuasive.

RaceName: Amodil Group Novices' Hurdle (ARC Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Promising March 2025 point bumper third (good), but hasn't lasted home in any run beyond 2m1f; needs 249-day break to have brought him on.

RaceName: Amodil Group Novices' Hurdle (ARC Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Fair third on sole Irish point start three years ago, but no evidence much ability remains on just three hurdles starts since.
Point winner who was runner-up in a bumper; not so good over hurdles, but that could change now handicapping and he might enjoy this nicer ground.
Point winner who is 0-11 over hurdles but, since returning from a break in March, she's performed well in three out of four handicaps; solid contender provided she takes to the new hood.

Best form has been at Tipperary where she won a bumper and was last seen finishing a 12l third on her handicap debut; that was in October, so there are concerns on the fitness front; some market confidence would be welcomed.
A market mover when an 11.5l fifth here in May and was 11-2 when brought down early next time; he's a better chaser than hurdler, but that's reflected in his marks.
Won a weak maiden hurdle at Roscommon last August; only 7l away in a handicap hurdle at Punchestown in May and cheekpieces are now added after a weak finish at Kilbeggan; has the ability to feature.
Flat wins in Britain and when with Gordon Elliott, for whom she went 0-9 over hurdles; has looked regressive on the Flat this year.
Has regressed sharply since winning on the Flat for Karl Burke and nothing positive to report so far over hurdles, including last week's handicap debut (33-1).
Has shown just enough in her last two maidens to believe she could have a future off this low a mark, now into handicaps; dropping back in trip probably no bad thing.
Beaten under 7l in two of his handicaps and off a lower mark now, so if the tongue-tie can trigger a revival, he could run well.
Won twice in Britain as a 2yo; had a mammoth absence prior to kicking off for current yard and has struggled to get anything going.

Performed as well as could have been expected in three Flat maidens last month and was beaten only a neck on handicap hurdle debut at Limerick in October (2m, yielding); 6lb higher than that day but he's one of the better options.
Essentially disappointing since initial promise in bumpers and left Gavin Cromwell under a cloud.
One notable run in a maiden last year, but that's beginning to look extremely flattering; no real form in handicaps.
Back-to-back handicap winner late last year and still on a competitive mark judged on last month's fourth of 16 at Sligo (2m1f, good); each-way claims.
Standout run over hurdles when third in a 2m4f maiden in 2023; beaten 16l in his last handicap hurdle, since when he's finished fifth in two points; opposable.
Back on his favoured good ground, he won well at Clonmel (2m) and a 5lb rise isn't the end of the world, having been competitive off this mark before.
Struggled in points and tailed off in all three maidens; much to prove on handicap debut.
Insufficient promise in maidens to believe she'll make an instant splash in handicaps.
Ran okay in a low-grade 1m5f handicap a month ago (blinkers returned) and there has been the odd bit of promise in handicap hurdles; 0-16 under both codes, but no forlorn hope here.
0-19 but only raced four times over hurdles and wasn't disgraced on handicap hurdle debut at Wexford (beaten 10l; 2m1f, good to yielding).
Dual Flat winner but 0-9 over hurdles and handicap form is deteriorating.
Things appeared to be picking up when second at Tramore (2m, good) but she weakened out of it at Sligo, finishing behind Theanniversary Man.
Placed off 7lb higher last year and some return to form when beaten 8l in the new blinkers at Wexford last week; remains to be seen if she builds on that.

110,000euros yearling; his standout effort from three runs so far was a win at Nottingham (6f, good) last month but he had a tough task in the Coventry at Royal Ascot last time; failed to beat a rival in that race but he's back in calmer waters now and could resume his progress at this new trip; not ruled out.

Big improvement upped to 7f on his third start when justifying favouritism in a Leicester maiden (good) last month; has more to do now switched to a nursery but that was a comfortable success and he's bred to be useful; respected.

Made winning debut at Haydock (6f, soft) in May; went down fighting when runner-up at Ffos Las (7.3f, good to soft) two weeks later but he was disappointing with visor added at Beverley last time; the headgear is now removed but opening mark looks on the high side and others are preferred.

New Bay filly who showed promise in her first two starts but her form really took off when beating 12 rivals at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) last month; showed a good attitude in that race and she's strongly respected off what looks a fair mark on this nursery debut.

Promising fifth on Leicester debut (5f, good to firm) in April and she built on that with a strong-finishing success in a maiden here (5.5f, good) the following month; returns after a break but she's closely related to a 7f 2yo winner and is open to more progress on this step up in trip; in the mix on nursery debut.

RaceName: Kilmurray Group Midlands National Handicap Chase (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Was in the form of his life in the spring and summer of last year, victory in this race being followed by an excellent fourth in the Galway Plate; by contrast, his spring form this year leaves plenty to be desired, but he is only 3lb higher than last year and cannot be dismissed with the stable in such form.

RaceName: Kilmurray Group Midlands National Handicap Chase (Listed Race)
Spotlight: A while now since he was at his very best with two big Leopardstown wins two seasons ago; well beaten in the Mayo National last time and looks best left alone at the present time.

RaceName: Kilmurray Group Midlands National Handicap Chase (Listed Race)
OwnerName: Patrick Corbett & Michael Corbett & J J Corbett & W Clancy
Spotlight: Several near misses before getting off the mark in spectacular fashion over 3m7f at Punchestown in April; up 12lb for that and extreme distances may well be what he wants, but this trip should be fine and he is a leading contender.

RaceName: Kilmurray Group Midlands National Handicap Chase (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Better chaser than hurdler and showed that when leaving some of these rivals behind in the Mayo National at Ballinrobe in May; 9lb looks a tolerable rise and he looks likely to play a leading role here; has won here over hurdles.

RaceName: Kilmurray Group Midlands National Handicap Chase (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Winning sequence came to an abrupt halt when pulled up at Limerick in the Munster National when last seen in October; better ground an obvious plus and not at all dismissed, despite the almost nine-month absence.

RaceName: Kilmurray Group Midlands National Handicap Chase (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Winning hurdler-chaser for Willie Mullins; fine run on his stable debut when fifth in the Kim Muir at Cheltenham; fell over banks at Punchestown; trip and ground are fine and has an each-way chance.

RaceName: Kilmurray Group Midlands National Handicap Chase (Listed Race)
OwnerName: Shanahan/Mrs M V Magnier/Mrs Carthy/Mrs Bartlett
Spotlight: Fair novice chaser before going pointing and hunter chasing; again showed his affinity for Killarney when winning a hunter chase there in May before disappointing at Listowel; more needed.

RaceName: Kilmurray Group Midlands National Handicap Chase (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Found two winnable opportunities in small-field novice chases at Down Royal and Punchestown; well beaten in a competitive novice handicap chase at the Punchestown festival in between; hard to assess in the context of this race, but looks the first string of the McManus quartet and is respected.

RaceName: Kilmurray Group Midlands National Handicap Chase (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Seven career wins but unable to make any impression behind Native Speaker at Ballinrobe or over 2m4f here last time; needs to up his game.

RaceName: Kilmurray Group Midlands National Handicap Chase (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Wide-margin winner of a Listowel handicap chase last year and confirmed his progression when winning the Killarney National in May; even with his 9lb penalty, he was well below par at Roscommon since; others preferred.

RaceName: Kilmurray Group Midlands National Handicap Chase (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Dual winner; no real match for Raglan Road in a three-runner contest at Punchestown last month; still relatively unexposed and not one to take lightly.

RaceName: Kilmurray Group Midlands National Handicap Chase (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Wide-margin winner of a staying handicap chase at Punchestown 14 months ago, but no real trace of that form since; switches from Willie Mullins to Gordon Elliott to try to improve his fortunes, but others preferred.

RaceName: Kilmurray Group Midlands National Handicap Chase (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Second in a C&D handicap chase last year; very solid efforts in Listed contests at Leopardstown and Punchestown before a disappointing effort in the Mayo National; a definite player if he comes back to his previous form.

Tom Hutsby was also up and claiming 10lb when nailed on the line here last month in a 2m7f class 3 that was no worse a race than this; respected, for all that hurdles mark has never higher following a 3lb raise; cheekpieces return.

Front-runner whose 2024 six-timer came over longer trips than today's; no evidence in three runs since a 14-month absence that he retains sufficient ability to figure here; gains cheekpieces and a tongue-tie.

Last five wins have all been gained over 2m4f, but three defeats since May's latest Uttoxeter success (her fifth there) have taken her score to 0-4 racing off marks in the 120s; rivalry for early lead.

Bidding to emulate stablemate Handlethekettle, who took the same C&D 0-140 as him last year before going on to add this race; 4lb rise for that latest win leaves him with nothing in hand on peak RPRs and he could still jump better, but there's nothing halfway as progressive in opposition.

OwnerName: DL Adams, JA Adams & G McPherson
Spotlight: Worcester record of 3122U1 includes last month's 2m7f win off 6lb lower (good); no margin for error back on his highest mark, but place claims if now able to return to the consistency of his 2024-25 campaign.

2m maiden win here last August (good) remains his only success in 11 over hurdles; debuted hood (retained here) possibly behind a better round of jumping than he sometimes manages around sharp tracks when going close off today's mark at Newton Abbot last month; place claims in same mood.

Generally fast-weakening efforts since returning from a winter break, so the recourse to wind surgery is unsurprising; mark never lower, but effectiveness down at 2m4f hasn't been tested since March 2024.

RaceName: Sign Up To Our Newsletter Mares Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Just two runs over hurdles in early 2025, winning a maiden and then outclassed in a Grade 2 (ground perhaps too testing); some competitive form on the Flat since then, but not last time in January.

RaceName: Sign Up To Our Newsletter Mares Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won her maiden on good ground and, back under similar conditions, she found only one too good at Clonmel (2m3f); solid chance off 1lb higher.

RaceName: Sign Up To Our Newsletter Mares Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Bumper winner; runner-up in a 2m3f maiden at Clonmel before not appearing to stay 2m6f next time; tongue-tie added for handicap debut and could be a factor.

RaceName: Sign Up To Our Newsletter Mares Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Came home 7l to the good in a 2m3f handicap here (good) before pulling up over nearly 3m when stamina was perhaps an issue; 9lb higher than for that breakthrough win, but wouldn't be exposed just yet.

RaceName: Sign Up To Our Newsletter Mares Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 2m2f maiden winner for previous yard; only 13-2 in a Listowel handicap on debut for this one, but she ended up tailed off; we know she's better than that and Jack Kennedy is a notable jockey booking.

RaceName: Sign Up To Our Newsletter Mares Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner in 2023; some good runs in the meantime - including over fences - and perhaps needed last month's comeback run at Punchestown (it looked that way).

RaceName: Sign Up To Our Newsletter Mares Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Sligo maiden hurdle winner who was then beaten about 20l in a handicap; has since raced three times over fences, not kicking on from a fair first effort; hard to make a solid case.

RaceName: Sign Up To Our Newsletter Mares Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Lightly raced; ran her best race yet when beaten 12l in a 2m5f maiden at Punchestown where she stayed on well after getting behind; open to improvement.

RaceName: Sign Up To Our Newsletter Mares Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Point winner; form figures of 443 in her handicap hurdles, beaten between about 3l and 18l; if holding her form, she might well be placed again.

RaceName: Sign Up To Our Newsletter Mares Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Pulled up in a handicap last time, but the ground was soft and her previous fourth of 15 in a maiden was here over 2m3f on good ground; possibilities off this mark, given the RPR she posted that day; cheekpieces added.

RaceName: Sign Up To Our Newsletter Mares Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: E Dillon & B Power & J J Whelan & K R Waplington
Spotlight: Has performed okay in modest 1m5f handicaps this year; runner-up in a maiden hurdle and heavy ground was against her in two handicaps; not dismissed.

RaceName: Sign Up To Our Newsletter Mares Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won one of her two points; slowly away when beaten 14l in a Sligo maiden last time out (2m1f, good); this mark isn't obviously lenient, but it's still early days.

RaceName: Sign Up To Our Newsletter Mares Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: No closer than 26l in her three handicaps and has only dropped 5lb for those.

RaceName: Sign Up To Our Newsletter Mares Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Some promise in one of her maidens, but tailed off on handicap debut which proved to be her final start for Gavin Cromwell.

RaceName: Sign Up To Our Newsletter Mares Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Eoghan Michael Wallis & Gerard Fintan Wallis
Spotlight: 0-13 but not disgraced in 2m/2m1f handicaps in April and she has each-way hopes.

RaceName: Sign Up To Our Newsletter Mares Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Only 2.5l away at Tramore two runs back but fared less well at Clonmel; now 0-16 and 3lb out of the weights.

Took form to new level when collecting good prize on AW in January, his 14th career success; below best on turf lately but might be perked up by a change of headgear here and is too well handicapped to rule out.

Ended last season with big-field win at York (1m2f, good) in the autumn and posted another career-best performance when leading close home over C&D (good) on reappearance outing in May; ought to have been suited by the good to soft ground when well beaten in valuable Espom handicap on Oaks day but still enters calculations on return to this track.

Very useful 3yo when based in France; largely disappointing for Joseph O'Brien in Ireland last year but started off for Tim Easterby with two encouraging runs at York (1m2f/1m) last month and has possibilities here.

Held her own in some hot handicaps after winning Newmarket maiden (1m2f, good) in May 2025 but has not really fired on either start this season; will appreciate this ease in grade but needs to up her game.
Unexposed 3yo who won tactical, three-runner Windsor novice (1m2f, good to firm) a fortnight ago and is now 2-3; contests much deeper race on this handicap debut but probably still has potential.
6-24 on AW but 0-12 on turf; recent efforts have been underwhelming and he's probably better suited by 1m4f; not the percentage call.

Won over C&D (good) just under a year ago and was also on target over the extended 1m4f here (good) in May; not quite at same level when fifth of eight over C&D a fortnight ago but there were some mitigating circumstances and she remains of interest.

Well-bred colt who delivered on earlier promise when readily drawing clear to justify odds-on favouritism in Pontefract novice (1m2f, good) in April, his third run; makes handicap debut after ten-week break but should still have a fair bit more to offer; respected.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Bumper winner for Nicky Henderson, but very lightly raced since and overall form does not leave much room for optimism.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Four-time winner over hurdles and has performed respectably over fences without winning; not a bad run in a 2m4f handicap here last time after seven months off and can go well.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Much the best of these over hurdles, probably a career-best effort when second in the race formerly known as the Coral Cup at Cheltenham in March; yet to reach that level over fences, but this looks his best opportunity to date.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Consistent performer over hurdles, but jumped poorly on his chase debut at Wexford in March and well beaten back over flights since at Down Royal; questions to answer.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Returned from the point-to-point circuit to run a decent race in a beginners' chase over 2m4f here last month; likely to stay well and could run into a place.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Dual winner over hurdles; yet to be quite as effective over fences and disappointed over 2m at Wexford last time; interesting stepped up in trip and could leave that form behind.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Two-time winner over hurdles; rated 12lb lower over fences and deservedly so on the book; remains capable of much better.

RaceName: Martin Keighley Racing Club Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Pulled up on first two hurdling starts for Paul Nicholls but seemed to be getting his act together when close third of five at Ffos Las (2m4f, good) in May; subsequently changed hands for £21,000; open to significant further progress in handicaps for new stable.

RaceName: Martin Keighley Racing Club Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Made jockey work hard and was no threat to winner when keeping on to snatch second from Estacas at Market Rasen (2m4f, good; first-time tongue-tie) last month and now 0-8 over hurdles; however, that was his first run since February and he's probably still on a workable mark; big player if he travels more sweetly in first-time cheekpieces.

RaceName: Martin Keighley Racing Club Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Returned from break with two low-key runs this spring but looked back in good nick when third here (2m, good) last month and will be fine over today's longer trip; one to consider.

RaceName: Martin Keighley Racing Club Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: 0-10 over hurdles but seemingly aided by tongue-tie on last two appearances, more recently when clear second at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good) last month; competes off same mark here and enters calculations.

RaceName: Martin Keighley Racing Club Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Opened British account under strong ride over C&D (good) in May and has obvious claims if judged on that performance but already looked beaten when badly hampered at third-last hurdle at Market Rasen last month and needs to bounce back.

RaceName: Martin Keighley Racing Club Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Unexposed after only five starts (one in a point) but was soundly beaten on recent stable/handicap debut at Stratford (2m6f, good to firm) and needs to take a big step forward.

RaceName: Martin Keighley Racing Club Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Well beaten here (2m, good) last month, after a break, and has now been unplaced all nine hurdle starts; might come on for the run but others have much more obvious claims; 1lb out of the weights.

RaceName: Follow Us On X Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Trainer likes him and latest outing was the first time he's shown why, backpedalling in second when last-flight faller at Punchestown; place chance if building on that in new tongue-tie.

RaceName: Follow Us On X Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Half-brother to smart staying chaser Lombron; ran okay in a point; 11s, was just behind AJP Falco when that one came down at the last in 3m maiden at Punchestown, finishing 16l third in the end.

RaceName: Follow Us On X Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Won Grade 2 bumper at the DRF in 2024; sidelined until unplaced run in Fairyhouse bumper at Easter; notably weak in the betting for hurdling debut at Ballinrobe, hampered early on and weakened late over extended 2m6f, finishing 5l off strong-finishing Young Doran; sharper for that experience, and probably fitter too.

RaceName: Follow Us On X Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: 5yo by Jack Hobbs; dam 1m7f-3m hurdle/chase winner, out of unraced half-sister to dual bumper winner Blue Buster; just mild promise in points in April/May.

RaceName: Follow Us On X Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Ordinary form in points; couple of fair efforts in maidens over 2m/2m4f, but hasn't looked to be crying out for this trip.

RaceName: Follow Us On X Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Point winner but modest form in bumpers and over hurdles; best watched for now.

RaceName: Follow Us On X Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Beat a useful type when winning a bumper and early hurdling efforts were promising; on third start after a year off, back to form when second (winner ran well off 115 since) over 2m4f at Listowel where he looked likely to drop away but plugged on; tongue-tie added.

RaceName: Follow Us On X Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Half-brother to three winners over distances from 2m3f-3m; 33-1 (from 100s) in extended 2m6f maiden at Ballinrobe, came from rear to finish second, around 5l in front of Jeroboam Machin; 8lb worse off with that rival, but open to plenty of improvement.

RaceName: Follow Us On X Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Court Cave mare, dam placed in 2m7f hurdle, family of smart jumper Highway One O One.

RaceName: Follow Us On X Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Held in points and modest form so far in bumpers/maiden hurdle.

RaceName: Follow Us On X Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: No sign of ability so far; change of headgear.

RaceName: Follow Us On X Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Point winner and bumper-placed; second to smart type over 2m at Navan; didn't quite see out 2m6f when third at Downpatrick last month; cheekpieces.

RaceName: Follow Us On X Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Court Cave half-sister to winners Macs Charm (useful 2m3f-3m hurdle/chase), Monatomic (useful 2m chase/2m5f hurdle) and Koori Star (2m2f hurdle); dam unraced half-sister to Scottish Grand National winner Win My Wings; runner-up in pair of points in November; after a break, wears tongue-tie for rules debut.

Two wins since joining Tony Carroll, the latest a small-field Class 4 at Chepstow (5f, good to soft) in May; solid effort from a less-than-ideal draw in the Epsom Dash five weeks ago; not discounted back down in class.

Ideally suited by going around a left-hand bend, with his six wins coming here and at Wolverhampton; 10lb higher than for his win in this race last year and the widest draw isn't a big selling point today; cheekpieces join the hood today.

Ended losing run with an emphatic success at Wolverhampton (5f) six weeks ago and the runner-up has done his bit for the form since; still seeking a first turf win though and following up off 7lb higher won't be easy.
Back to winning ways with his Class 5 win at Pontefract (5f, good to soft) in May; respectable efforts twice since but he would be more appealing on slower ground.
Prominent racer who has run two good races at Hamilton (6f, good and 5f, good to soft) this season; unraced on faster than good but a key player if underfoot conditions are suitable.

Took advantage of a lowly mark at Southwell in December and he has flourished since, adding two more AW wins over the winter and going in again at Salisbury (6f, soft) last month; second in two fast-ground handicaps since; should remain competitive but this test will be sharp enough.

Underwent a wind operation after his seasonal return in May and he has run good races in both starts since, second over 6f here (good to soft) and then third at Doncaster (6f, good to firm) three weeks ago; back up in class today but not discounted.
Course win (5f, good) last July; arrives in top form, winning Class 5 events at Bath and Newbury (5f, firm and good to firm) this summer; 2lb rise shouldn't prevent another bold bid.

Two Flat wins in Germany early in his career, and Listed-placed; can be rated a possible future hurdles winner based on Punchestown fifth last month on handicap debut; slight reservation now on account of a suspicion that he might prefer a longer trip.
Fair form, without winning, in bumpers and maiden hurdles; unseated at the first at Wexford in May, so effectively no run since last October; form includes a 14l second in a 2m course maiden.
One of her better efforts on latest when fourth at Wexford; needs to raise her game; strong trainer/rider combination; worth a market check.

Finished in mid-field in a strong Leopardstown maiden on debut; remote sixth behind Bambino Fever at Fairyhouse in January, made mistakes and fell two out at Naas in February; beaten favourite in two maidens in recent months; Sligo third was respectable, and the bar is set low for her handicap debut
1-28 over hurdles; soundly beaten in races at Ballinrobe and Downpatrick since a breakthrough win at Sligo; others preferred.
Took a sharp rise in the ratings for a smooth Downpatrick win; met trouble when favourite at Bellewstown last week; her fourth placing (beaten 10l) can be upgraded a little.
Nothing obvious in three maiden hurdle runs last year to suggest an immediate impact in handicaps.
Off for almost two years after winning at Thurles in 2022; returns now after another long absence; trainer has a stronger candidate here in Chesterbaylad.
Useful on the Flat for Joseph O'Brien; modest hurdles form apart from a shock win at Ballinrobe in May 2024; tailed off at Wexford in May after a winter break.

Long-priced winner on yielding ground at Down Royal in December; may have needed Bellewstown run in April; fair effort at 33-1 at Sligo a month ago, not far behind third-placed Granlahan despite not getting the best of runs.
Promoted to third at Limerick in May after the winner's disqualification; followed that with a fair third at Sligo; hampered and unseated at the sixth at Bellewstown last week.
Remote fourth in a conditions race here last month; her single bumper run (at Tramore last August) was satisfactory; unconvincing hurdles form.
Weak form in maidens; pulled up in handicap outings over longer trips than this at Tramore in May and this venue last month.
Ran three times in maidens in the first half of last year; no sign of worthwhile form.
Showed next to nothing in maidens; beaten a long way in sixth in a 2m4f Limerick contest on handicap debut; hard to fancy

Attracted support at Down Royal in early May, let down by his jumping; much more like it when second in a 19-runner contest at Punchestown nine days later; failed to build on that at this venue next time, this longer trip may suit him better.

RaceName: Martin Keighley Racing Club Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Dual 2m course winner in early part of last season; has mixed record since and is not yet proven over this far but seemed to have benefited from a wind operation when close third of five here (2m, good) last month.

RaceName: Martin Keighley Racing Club Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Ex-French 5yo; began British career with some disappointing hurdle/chase runs this spring but drew clear to score by 7l here (2m7f hurdle, good) nine days ago; escapes a penalty for that and is 8lb ahead of the handicapper; makes obvious appeal.

RaceName: Martin Keighley Racing Club Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Won twice last summer (2m7f/3m2f) but returned from a break with two disappointing runs last month (both 2m7f) and this drop back in trip is not an obvious move.

RaceName: Martin Keighley Racing Club Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Free-goer who won twice at Bangor last year (2m3f/2m7f); absent since consecutive heavy defeats last July/September, though, and returns with quite a bit to prove.

RaceName: Martin Keighley Racing Club Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Without a win since 2023 and was out of form when last seen over hurdles in the autumn; this year's Flat form has been fairly respectable but he's on the downgrade and makes only limited appeal back in this sphere.

RaceName: Martin Keighley Racing Club Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Tailed off on handicap debut (2m4f; wore cheekpieces) in February and probably didn't achieve a great deal when sixth at Southwell (3m, good; blinkered) last month but remains lightly raced and may yet burst into life.

RaceName: Martin Keighley Racing Club Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Dropped out and was beaten about 50l on stable/handicap debut at Ffos Las (2m4f, good) in April but remains unexposed and is worth keeping an eye on in the betting; had wind operation in May; tongue-tie removed after one use.

Eight wins over hurdles and going for three in a row in this race, landing previous two renewals under this jockey; beat Reverend Hubert by a head last year; after eight months off, comeback third in Killarney chase was a good effort; quiet since but can't rule him out.

Stable stalwart not getting any better at this stage, but solid hurdling form last two completions, when beaten 10l in the Stayers' at Cheltenham and a similar distance in 24-runner Punchestown festival handicap; won a pair of conditions hurdles at Thurles last year; best going this way around and genuine good ground would see him in with a big shout.

Smart dual-purpose type won a nice Flat prize over 2m a fortnight ago; came off worse in a couple of 3m clashes with Happy Jacky last year, but not by much, including when beaten a head in the corresponding race; relishes good ground.
Grand sort has won four hurdles, including two small-field contests here last year; returned with a spin over fences in May; 2m3f is the farthest he's gone, so stamina for 3m is the concern.

OwnerName: Anthony N Durkan & B M Durkan
Spotlight: Had a productive time last year, winning a hurdle and two chases; stays this trip well, but has been off since last August and this looks like a prep for Galway, where he has run well before.

Course winner has been a good flagbearer for yard; latest hurdling effort was creditable 8l eighth in Martin Pipe in March; fair runs in beginners' chases since, over 3m1f last time; bit to find but mightn't be far away.
By some way the lowest rated and is out of form; no chance in this company; visor.

Three runs earlier this year weren't great but he won three times last year, twice off breaks of 100+ days; stays just about this far and that record when fresh means a big run could be in the offing, even though he'll need a few to underperform to win.

OwnerName: D G Pryde & D P Van Der Hoeven & G McGladery & J Beaumont
Spotlight: Smashing old boy won a cross-country chase at Craon last September, and the year before as well; this first hurdles run since 2022 is most likely a prep for another trip to France.

Mare won four times in 2024, including a pair of hurdles; winless since, but creditable efforts over fences last twice including fourth of 16 in Mayo National; trip and ground okay and has a chance at the weights.

Sole win from 13 starts was in a four-runner novice at Ascot (7f, soft) in September 2024; well handicapped on his old form but he's been well held in his last five runs and has something to prove after another break.

Four-time AW winner who returned to form on his second run after wind surgery when a front-running second at Southwell (7f, Tapeta) last month; record of 0-14 on turf but he's 6lb lower than last time and is well handicapped on his second at York last spring; respected.

Recorded his fourth turf win when making all over 7f at Musselburgh (good) 18 days ago; has wide draw on his belated Chester debut but he's only 2lb higher than last time and is a player if he can repeat that form.

Placed at Wolverhampton in her first two starts this spring and same story when third in a C&D maiden (good) in May; should have more to offer now switched to a handicap but this opening mark is no gift and she's not had much luck with the draw.
Seven-time AW winner but sole turf win was in 2023 and he was tailed off back in this sphere at Catterick (7f, good) in May; has something to prove after another break and others are preferred.

OwnerName: Berkshire Parts & Panels Ltd No1 Fanclub
Spotlight: Won a C&D maiden (good) last July but he's been well held in three handicaps this season including over 7.6f here (soft) last month; still lightly raced but he needs to rediscover his spark back on quicker ground.

Not a straightforward customer but he got back on the scoresheet with a last-gasp success at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) 12 days ago; has a penalty but he won off a higher mark at Doncaster last spring and he looks worth another try at this trip; dangerous.

Made the breakthrough with cheekpieces added at Redcar (7f, good) in May and he backed that up with a close third of 15 at Doncaster last month; up another 2lb but he still has low mileage and is open to more progress in 7f handicaps; interesting contender.
Useful 2yo for Joseph O'Brien but he struggled last season at trips ranging from 5f to 2m; has dropped a long way in the weights but he's now 1-16 and needs a major revival after 202 days off.

No win since October 2024 but he's on a reduced mark and showed signs of a revival when fourth at Thirsk (6f, good; 11-1) last month; has possibilities if he can build on that and he's effective at this trip.

0-26 but he's been runner-up five times and was a creditable third in a maiden handicap at Chepstow (7f, good to firm) two weeks ago; could be in the mix again but he has some work to do from 3lb out of the weights.

RaceName: New Forest Estate & Golf Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won over 2m4f at Thurles in 2024 for Philip Rothwell, his only success from 28 starts; placed nine times; in fair form before changing stables; market may provide clues to what is expected on his first start for Gordon Elliott.

RaceName: New Forest Estate & Golf Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Absent since scoring over an extended 2m6f at Ballinrobe early last season; an unknown quantity over this trip; placed over 2m on the Flat; fair chance of staying this far over hurdles at this circuit.

RaceName: New Forest Estate & Golf Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Single-figure odds on both starts for this stable after a long layoff; needs to raise his game, but possibly rash to ignore.

RaceName: New Forest Estate & Golf Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: C&D winner in September 2022; last success was at Listowel the following year; at the veteran stage now; returned to form when second at Clonmel in April; flopped at Sligo in May, raising a doubt.

RaceName: New Forest Estate & Golf Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Maiden who looks capable of minor success judged on a pair of seconds in handicap hurdles at Clonmel in March and May last year; signed off 2025 with two fair runs in handicap chases; lack of a recent run is a possible negative.

RaceName: New Forest Estate & Golf Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Lost form drastically after a Clonmel win in January on the 101st outing of his career; pulled up on last four starts; Love Like This is yard's main hope.

RaceName: New Forest Estate & Golf Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Built on a promising second at Limerick when winning over C&D last month, his first hurdles win to add to a Tramore chase success; had a talented 7lb claimer aboard at Limerick, talented 5lb claimer this time.

RaceName: New Forest Estate & Golf Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Had plenty to spare when scoring a breakthrough win over an extended 2m6f at Limerick last week; a wind operation seems to have done the trick, and she is suited by good ground; can go well again off 9lb higher.

RaceName: New Forest Estate & Golf Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Her single win and all three places have come at Downpatrick; put in a pleasing effort when third there last month; however, overall form indicates she is not guaranteed to reproduce her best form elsewhere.

RaceName: New Forest Estate & Golf Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Running for the 45th time when opening her account with a C&D win last month; has gone up only 5lb , but probably vulnerable given her overall profile.

RaceName: New Forest Estate & Golf Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Runner-up in a point; modest form in maiden hurdles; has made the frame in two Clonmel handicaps; cheekpieces on now; good 5lb claimer booked.

RaceName: New Forest Estate & Golf Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Hints of ability in handicap hurdles last year, and well backed on a few occasions; below par at Cork on final start before joining Emmet Mullins; the market may provide a guide.

RaceName: New Forest Estate & Golf Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 17-race maiden over hurdles; best form at Wexford; fell at fifth in the race won by Knocknagoran Lady at Limerick last week.

RaceName: New Forest Estate & Golf Club Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Mrs Peter Casey & Mrs Paula Casey
Spotlight: Not a bad run at Limerick on her second run after a long break; reportedly bled at Downpatrick last time; never shorter than 25-1, and hard to fancy now.

RaceName: New Forest Estate & Golf Club Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Signs of improvement in two handicap outings last October, notably when a staying-on sixth over a similar trip to this at Down Royal; half-brother to five Flat winners, including multiple Pattern scorer Tullius; should be more mature than when last seen; each-way prospect if fit.

Not one to rely upon that heavily but scored off today's mark at Huntingdon (2m, good) in March and creditable third of 14 at Stratford (2m, good to firm) 11 days ago; features prominently in calculations.

OwnerName: John Murray & Exor Of Late Niall Farrell
Spotlight: Well beaten after racing too freely at Perth (2m4f, good to soft) in May and now 0-9 over hurdles; hasn't been with current stable long and may benefit from this drop back in trip but others look safer.

Won against own age group on soft ground in the autumn and close second on handicap debut at Kempton (2m, good) in March; below that form behind Best Night at Stratford recently but might just have needed that outing and will be a threat if back on song in first-time cheekpieces.

Won a bumper in spring 2025 but has made a disappointing start to her jumping career; all three handicap runs were over fences, with two non-completions sandwiching a 33l defeat; reverts to hurdling with quite a bit to prove.
Unexposed after only four hurdle runs but was safely held when upped to 2m4f for last month's handicap debut; drops back in trip in need of improvement.

Well held when third at Warwick (2m, good) last month, after a break, but gained her wins in the last two editions of this race and is now 2lb lower than last year; likely to have been primed for a bold defence.

1-11 over hurdles; returned from break with a quite encouraging fourth at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good) last month and subsequent run may be best overlooked (pulled up after losing action in early stages) but others appeal more all the same.
Unplaced all seven hurdle starts and absent since pulled up twice here (2m4f/2m) for Ryan Potter last June; 14lb wrong for stable debut; can't be recommended.

RaceName: Racing Again July 24th Fillies INH Flat Race
OwnerName: David Spratt & Kevin McMunigal & Mark Hopkins
Spotlight: Basically Flat-bred, although dam was useful jumper; better of two runs in easy-ground maiden hurdles when fourth at Limerick; disappointing favourite at Tramore in April.

RaceName: Racing Again July 24th Fillies INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Was nibbled into 16s when running well at Tramore, beaten 11l, and both second and third were runners-up next time; top rider takes over.

RaceName: Racing Again July 24th Fillies INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Bit of promise on Leopardstown debut in March, but twice soundly beaten on better ground in May; long way behind Vadique most recently.

RaceName: Racing Again July 24th Fillies INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Only tenth of 14, but only beaten 10l, when 18-1 at Kilbeggan; good bit more needed.

RaceName: Racing Again July 24th Fillies INH Flat Race
Spotlight: By Irish St Leger winner Flag Of Honour; half-sister to point/2m4f hurdle winner Western Clouds, family of smart staying chaser Vintage Clouds.

RaceName: Racing Again July 24th Fillies INH Flat Race
Spotlight: By Highland Reel out of unraced mare; sister to smart 1m4f winner Highland Bling and half-sister to two other winners.

RaceName: Racing Again July 24th Fillies INH Flat Race
Spotlight: By Affinisea out of 2m4f hurdles winner; half-sister to useful bumper winner Fascile Mode (also won over hurdles and fences); scratched due to soft ground a month ago.

RaceName: Racing Again July 24th Fillies INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Homebred by the Marigas; Nathaniel filly out of unraced half-sister to Big Buck's and useful French 2m3f-2m6f hurdle/chase winner Buck's Bank; these owners don't have many that lack ability.

RaceName: Racing Again July 24th Fillies INH Flat Race
Spotlight: By Order Of St George; dam runner-up in only point start, half-sister to smart bumper and 2m-2m4f hurdle winner Black Tears.

RaceName: Racing Again July 24th Fillies INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Old Persian half-sister to a couple of Flat winners; dam 7f winner, half-sister to Italian 6f Group 3 winner Victory Laurel; hood on for debut.

RaceName: Racing Again July 24th Fillies INH Flat Race
Spotlight: 40s, tailed off at Listowel in May.

RaceName: Racing Again July 24th Fillies INH Flat Race
Spotlight: By Elusive Pimpernel; half-sister to winners Not Staying Long (2m3f-3m1f hurdle/chase), Barden Bella (2m-3m3f chase), Track And Trace (2m5f-3m6f hurdle/chase inc US) and Hard Dealt (point/2m4f hurdle); dam half-sister to useful jumpers Sempo and Aqua Dude; yard 10-96 in bumpers last five seasons, rider yet to score.

RaceName: Racing Again July 24th Fillies INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Ran a cracker for small yard when 100-1 for good-ground maiden hurdle at Clonmel, third of 17, albeit field was decimated by early incidents; second won a bumper next time.

RaceName: Racing Again July 24th Fillies INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Homebred by Capri; dam unraced half-sister to German 1m3f/1m7f winner; yard going well but wouldn't be known for bumper winners.

RaceName: Racing Again July 24th Fillies INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Beaten 14l by odds-on winner with solid form when second (4-1) at Listowel in May; probably didn't have much of note behind her, but looked very green and can improve.

RaceName: Racing Again July 24th Fillies INH Flat Race
Spotlight: By Westerner; dam placed in a point and 2m-2m5f hurdles, closely related to 3m hurdle winner Tenzing, out of half-sister to high-class jumper Blaklion; yard 8-32 in bumpers last five seasons, and young rider was aboard one of those here in April; hood on for debut.
Habitual front-runner; reeled in after going off a bit too hard over 1m at Brighton recently but won over 7f there (good to firm) two starts ago and has the ideal draw here; could take some catching.
1-20 for Julie Camacho, the win gained over 1m on Tapeta in September 2024; makes stable debut from workable mark and with trip/ground suitable but absent since two below-par AW runs in the winter.

OwnerName: J Sarkar, D Sarkar, O Sarkar & D Sarkar
Spotlight: Dual 7f winner last summer (good to firm/AW); made low-key seasonal debut over 6f last month and not ideally drawn here but still quite lightly raced, and that recent outing ought to have brought him on.
Third at Newbury (1m, good) on latest turf start in May and also ran well when filling same position at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) 12 days ago but wide draw makes things awkward here.

Gained all four wins over sprint trips, the latest in September 2024; ran quite well over 7f at Catterick (good) in May, though, and nearly exploited this career-low mark at Doncaster (6f, good to firm; second run in cheekpieces) a week ago; due to go back up 3lb; must be considered.
Three-time AW winner; 0-10 on turf but has made the frame off today's mark twice in recent weeks (6f/7f, good to firm) and has good draw here; probably won't be far away.
Six-race maiden; placed over 7f on soft ground as a 2yo but was always behind on seasonal/handicap debut at Beverley (7.4f, good to soft) in April and will need a much more dynamic performance here.
A maiden after nine runs but has gradually worked his way back to form this season and kept on pretty well for third at Carlisle (7f, good) last month; could have a say.

Lightly raced filly, absent since second of four behind well-handicapped rival on nursery debut at Ffos Las (6f, good) just under a year ago; up in trip for stable debut (same owner); well drawn; market helpful.

Placed at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) in April and might have been stretched by 1m when respectable fourth at Redcar (good) last month but has mixed record for this stable since September and is drawn on the outside.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Seven-time chase winner who developed into a useful performer last season; below form at Uttoxeter recently and tricky to weigh up over hurdles but still looks tempting, given he's 27lb lower in this sphere.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles but returned from a break with a pretty good second at Market Rasen (2m4f, good; refitted cheekpieces) on Sunday and is quickly back in action before handicapper has chance to nudge him up the weights; unexposed as a stayer; likely contender.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Seemingly aided by cheekpieces when making the frame at Warwick twice this season (3m1f/3m2f, good) but his finishing was disappointing in those races and he's patently not as good as he was; others preferred.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: DL Adams, JA Adams & G McPherson
Spotlight: Won over 2m3f on hurdle debut in April 2025 but lightly raced and disappointing since; positives can be drawn from last month's reappearance sixth at Southwell (3m, good) but he needs to have come on for the run.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won three times under Sean Bowen (once over C&D) in 2025; well below best on all four appearances this year but has slipped to a good mark and the champion jockey evidently still has faith in him; revival possible.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Came good at Warwick (3m1f, good) in May, his seventh start over hurdles, but beaten about 11l when fourth on next two outings (one over C&D) and needs to kick on again.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles but was making first appearance for 13 months when keeping on for third at Southwell (2m4f, good) in May and is sure to be suited by this move back up in trip; shortlisted.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 2-13 over hurdles, the latter win gained off this mark over C&D (good) last summer; has lost his way a bit since, though, and was safely held off his lower chase mark last month; others have more pressing claims.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-6 under rules; struggled on chasing debut in March but made a promising stable debut when fourth over hurdles at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good) last month and will probably be suited by this move back up in trip; a possible.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles and 0-12 overall; ended string of dismal efforts and travelled well for a long way when clear second at Ffos Las (3m, good to soft) in May but is effectively 6lb higher from out of the weights here.
